title: Final Oscars Predictions – Best Film Editing - Filmotomy
text: Filmotomy's final Oscars predictions for Best Film Editing, released before the ceremony, listed the contenders in order of likelihood to win: One Battle After Another (Andy Jurgensen) as the top prediction; Sinners (Michael P. Shawver) as a strong contender; F1 (Stephen Mirrione) with possible upset potential; Sentimental Value (Olivier Bugge Coutté) with historical precedent; and Marty Supreme (Ronald Bronstein, Josh Safdie) with frenetic editing style. Filmotomy noted that the editing category typically aligns with Best Picture winners, making the prediction complex. The analysts considered the strength of supporting categories such as Sound Design and Cinematography in their assessment. Filmotomy's prediction proved accurate with One Battle After Another ultimately winning the award.
